Seri Mutiara Berhad — Internal Incident Report (Operational/Cyber) — Confidential; not for external release or analyst briefings prior to an immediate announcement on the Exchange.
On 02/06/2026 at approximately 03:10 MYT, our vendor SolarSafe Cloud Sdn Bhd detected anomalous authentication attempts against our hosted Warehouse Management System instance; a ransomware payload was quarantined and lateral movement contained within a segregated staging environment.
Impact summary: the Medan Tembusu warehouse experienced a 9-hour WMS outage; c.11% of daily outbound shipments by Gemilang Logistics Sdn Bhd were deferred; no physical damage or safety incidents reported.
Preliminary financial assessment indicates RM2–3 million revenue deferral into Q3, with potential margin impact from overtime and expedited freight; on an issuer-relative basis this may be material for the Logistics segment and could trigger an immediate announcement requirement under the Main Market Listing Requirements (Bursa Nusantara Securities Berhad) equivalent of paragraph 9.03A.
Forensics led by Perisai Digital Consulting Sdn Bhd finds no evidence of bulk exfiltration; two employee records (names and emails) may have been accessed—PDPA obligations apply and HR will issue notices within 72 hours; no customer PII or payment data were exposed.
Closed period: the Company enters a closed period for Q2 results on 01/07/2026; directors and principal officers must not deal in Seri Mutiara Berhad shares; this document contains non-public, price-sensitive information and is subject to insider trading prohibitions.
Disclosure plan: draft an immediate announcement for submission to the Exchange by 07:30 tomorrow; avoid selective disclosure—no investor, lender, or customer briefings until the announcement is released on the Exchange’s website.
Public references for context only: (i) our Sustainability Statement 2025 (public) disclosed ongoing cyber control enhancements; (ii) announcement dated 12/03/2026 titled “Operational Update: Flood Remediation at Medan Tembusu” (public) is unrelated to this incident.
Non-issuer operational updates: yesterday’s national telecom routing disruption reported in the press is a third‑party event; while it may have exacerbated latency, it remains general market information and not specific to the issuer.
Vendor communications: SolarSafe Cloud’s status page post is public and generic; under our contract, the vendor may not identify Seri Mutiara without consent.
Contingencies: if further analysis elevates materiality (e.g., cumulative impact above RM5 million at Group level), we will accelerate disclosure and request the Exchange to note a potential price‑sensitive development to mitigate unusual market activity queries.
Governance: the Risk Committee chaired by Puan Nadira Halim met at 16:00; Internal Audit to test privileged access and backup restore time objectives; a follow‑up determination on materiality is due by 20:00.
Staff reminder: discussing specifics of this incident on social media, messaging apps, or with counterparties before public release constitutes selective disclosure and may breach capital markets laws and policy CP‑04.
Contact: enquiries to Encik Farid Azlan, Head of Investor Relations, ir@serimutiara.example.my; media queries to corpcomms@serimutiara.example.my.
Note: this report supersedes a 10:30 draft that incorrectly estimated RM10–12 million impact; that estimate was withdrawn upon reconciliation and must not be relied upon.
